title: The Adventures of Toto – Flashcard Concept Mastery
author: Ruskin Bond

questions.question: Who bought Toto and from whom?
questions.correct_answer: Grandfather bought Toto from a tonga-driver for five rupees.
questions.explanation: Grandfather saw the monkey tied to a feeding trough and decided to add him to his private zoo.

questions.question: Why did Grandfather buy Toto?
questions.correct_answer: Because he felt the monkey looked out of place and wanted to keep him in his private zoo.
questions.explanation: Grandfather loved animals and often brought new pets home.

questions.question: What made Toto look pretty?
questions.correct_answer: His bright mischievous eyes, white teeth, and long tail.
questions.explanation: The narrator describes Toto as a pretty monkey with sparkling eyes and a useful tail.

questions.question: Why was Toto kept secretly at first?
questions.correct_answer: Because Grandmother did not like new animals being brought home.
questions.explanation: Grandfather and the narrator hid Toto until Grandmother was in a good mood.

questions.question: What did Toto do in the closet?
questions.correct_answer: He tore the wallpaper and destroyed the narrator’s blazer.
questions.explanation: Toto escaped from the peg and caused damage in the room.

questions.question: Why did the ticket collector call Toto a dog?
questions.correct_answer: Because he considered Toto an animal that required a fare like a dog.
questions.explanation: The ticket collector classified Toto as a dog and charged three rupees.

questions.question: Who was Nana?
questions.correct_answer: Nana was the family donkey that lived in the stable.
questions.explanation: Toto shared the stable with Nana but often troubled her.

questions.question: How did Toto take a bath?
questions.correct_answer: He tested the water and slowly stepped in until he was neck deep.
questions.explanation: Toto imitated the narrator and enjoyed bathing in warm water.

questions.question: How did Toto almost boil himself alive?
questions.correct_answer: He got into a kettle of warm water placed on the fire for tea.
questions.explanation: The water gradually heated up, and Toto kept jumping until Grandmother rescued him.

questions.question: Why was Toto finally sold back to the tonga-driver?
questions.correct_answer: Because his mischievous behaviour caused too much damage in the house.
questions.explanation: The family could not afford the frequent loss of dishes, clothes, and curtains.

notes.title: Toto’s Mischievous Nature
notes.content: Toto is curious and playful, but his constant mischief causes trouble for the family.

notes.title: Grandfather’s Love for Animals
notes.content: Grandfather enjoys collecting animals and keeping them in his private zoo.

facts.fact: Toto was bought for five rupees.
The story is written by Ruskin Bond and is known for its humour and simple narration.
